one_line_thesis: Lilly is the clearest large-cap beneficiary of the GLP-1/GIP obesity and diabetes cycle.

deep_dive.executive_summary:
Lilly is the clearest large-cap beneficiary of the GLP-1/GIP obesity and diabetes cycle. FY2025 operating cash flow was $16.8 billion; after $7.8 billion of capital spending, owner cash is about $9.98 per share. Q1 2026 revenue was $19.8 billion (+55.5% year over year), with net income $7.4 billion (diluted earnings per share $8.26).

At about $1,187 (Yahoo Finance, 2026-07-10), the Lawrence base case implies about -7.3% per year on normalized owner cash, below our 15% accumulate hurdle. The business has a widening durable advantage (Munger moat) on tirzepatide clinical data and manufacturi...

decision.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

valuation.interpretation: value_per_share is withheld while any material additive component is unpriced; priced_components_per_share is not a complete security value.

scenario_contract.rule: Cases must change cited causal operating, capital, probability, timing, or financing drivers—not only a terminal multiple.

change_control.rule: A source fact is locked. Every assumption change requires a reason, author, timestamp, and before/after value.

optionality.rule: Every option requires a beneficiary, probability, timing, remaining capital, failure case, and non-overlap control; absence of option value must be explicit rather than unvalued.

committee.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

method_fit.label: High-return compounder
applicability_tests: Owner earnings can be separated from stock compensation, working capital, and maintenance capital.
Incremental returns on retained capital are observable and durable.
The competitive advantage explains pricing, retention, share, or cost outcomes.
Terminal value is supported by a reinvestment runway rather than an unexplained multiple.
failure_modes: growth projected without its capital cost
terminal value unsupported by a durable mechanism

validation_cohort.purpose: Test depletion, commodity exposure, finite reserves, and royalty-unit reconciliation.
Test tangible capital, credit losses, funding, regulation, and excess-capital realization.
Test owner earnings, capital-light reinvestment, cyclicality, and buyback value creation.
Test normalized margins, industry supply response, replacement cost, and reinvestment timing.
Test product concentration, pipeline probabilities, patent cliffs, and catalyst timing.

method_fit.rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

outcomes.weighting_rule: Do not change a persona's relevance within a power zone until at least 20 attributable, dividend-aware outcomes exist there; never reweight automatically.

attribution.explanation: A complete security value is intentionally withheld while material components are unpriced. Attribution restarts after the first proof-complete baseline.

total_return_panel.error: Vendor events are discovery evidence; primary-source completeness has not been established.
total_return_panel.return_contract: split-adjusted close excluding dividends; event amounts normalized for later splits; cash reinvested at ex-date close; pre-tax nominal

coverage.note: Vendor events are discovery evidence; primary-source completeness has not been established.

valuation_decision.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

personas.label: Joel Greenblatt

timeline.label: Net income moved +94.9% versus the prior period (10,590.0 to 20,640.0).
Q1 2026 revenue $19.8B (+55.5% YoY); Mounjaro global $8.7B and Zepbound $4.2B in quarter signal continued tirzepatide momentum
FDA approved Kisunla (donanemab) for early Alzheimer's disease, adding a neuroscience growth leg beyond cardiometabolic
Zepbound U.S. revenue reached $13.5B in FY2025; obesity indication transformed Lilly into dual diabetes/obesity leader alongside Mounjaro
Capital spending rose to $7.8B (+55% YoY) to expand injectable and oral manufacturing capacity for GLP-1 demand
Mounjaro global revenue exceeded $22B combined U.S./international; tirzepatide became largest product franchise
FDA approved Zepbound (tirzepatide) for chronic weight management, opening the U.S. obesity market
FDA approved Mounjaro (tirzepatide) for type 2 diabetes, beginning the GLP-1/GIP dual-agonist cycle
Trulicity peaked as prior-generation GLP-1; Lilly pivoted R&D spend toward tirzepatide and next-wave cardiometabolic pipeline
Accelerated share repurchases and dividend growth as legacy Humalog/Alimta erosion required portfolio renewal
David Ricks continued as CEO; organization reoriented capital toward cardiometabolic and oncology pipeline under R&D chief Dan Skovronsky
Divested Elanco animal health to focus on human pharmaceuticals; proceeds funded internal R&D and bolt-on acquisitions

industry.structure: Global branded pharmaceuticals in cardiometabolic and oncology are oligopolistic: a handful of large incumbents compete on clinical data, manufacturing scale, and payer access; GLP-1 class has exceptionally high barriers due to capacity constraints and trial evidence.
industry.share_shift: Eli Lilly is gaining share in injectable GLP-1/GIP with tirzepatide (Mounjaro/Zepbound) while Novo Nordisk retains semaglutide leadership; Trulicity and legacy insulin products lose share.
industry.trend: Obesity and diabetes TAM expansion, oral GLP-1 development races, U.S. Inflation Reduction Act price negotiation, and multi-billion-dollar manufacturing capex super-cycles define the sector through 2028.

top_claims.claim: Net income moved +94.9% versus the prior period (10,590.0 to 20,640.0).
Basic EPS moved +95.6% versus the prior period (11.8 to 23.0).
Net income growth moved from +475.3% to +168.1% YoY for two consecutive quarters. Trailing-twelve-month growth confirms the same direction. Source: SEC XBRL quarterly filings.

risk_claims.claim: *Primary risk: Tirzepatide growth decelerates or margins compress before the capex cycle converts to normalized free cash flow, leaving investors holding a premium multiple on peak growth expectations.
Competition: Novo Nordisk and oral GLP-1 developers can erode share or force pricing concessions (10-K risk factors).
Manufacturing and supply: Capacity constraints have been a bottleneck; execution risk remains on new site ramp (10-K; capex $7.8B FY25).
